label: hate
rationale: Collective attribution of homicidal intent to an entire religious group: 'Muslims will not stop until they kill them all'.

label: hate
rationale: Uses a derogatory ethnic marker and slur ('Nilotic Foreskin wielding retards') to attack a community collectively.

label: offensive
rationale: Generic political insult ('THUG in chief') aimed at politicians, not an ethnic group.

label: neither
rationale: Political advice discussing ethnic political regions (Luo Nyanza, Waluhya) without contempt.

label: neither
rationale: Political criticism addressing members of an ethnic group ('Any Kikuyu who follows him') but not attacking the group as a whole.

label: neither
rationale: Discussing the political 'lethality' of voter blocs, an ordinary political comparison without ethnic contempt.

label: neither
rationale: Asking for evidence of political attacks, completely benign.

label: neither
rationale: Robust ethnic-bloc political talk regarding power dynamics ('stooge of Kikuyus to restore their ethnic domination'), reading as tribal arithmetic rather than hate.

label: neither
rationale: Long political analysis of ethnic voting blocs and political strategy, attributing ruthlessness to a voter base in a political context rather than inciting hatred.

label: neither
rationale: Banter about political loyalty ('Mulembe nation si wajinga'), no hate.

label: offensive
rationale: Quoting abusive language ('akili hawana, ni mafala') directed at a politician, not an ethnic group.

label: neither
rationale: Ordinary political commentary on a politician's relationship with their ethnic base.

label: neither
rationale: Political demand regarding 'Zionists', a political ideology, rather than a protected characteristic.

label: neither
rationale: Tribal arithmetic and political commentary ('Kikuyus aren't stupid').

label: offensive
rationale: Personal insult ('akili ya wamunyoro') implying low intelligence, directed at an individual rather than expressing group hate.

label: neither
rationale: Criticising an ethnic voting bloc ('Murima') for perceived tribalism ('ukabila ndio inawasumbua'), which is robust political criticism.

label: hate
rationale: Calls for the expulsion of a racial group ('operation rudisha mzungu ulaya'), matching the NCIC standard for hate.

label: neither
rationale: Criticising politicians for being tribalists, no hate expressed.

label: neither
rationale: Criticising a political/militant organisation (Hamas) and propaganda, not attacking an ethnic group.

label: offensive
rationale: Personal abuse ('jezebel zero brain') aimed at an individual.

label: neither
rationale: Ordinary political discourse and ethnic voting predictions ('Luos will support...').

label: hate
rationale: Threatens to impose political rule over an ethnic group through violence ('whether KIKUYUS like it or NOT -By FIRE/BULLET').

label: offensive
rationale: Personal insult ('thief') directed at an individual politician.

label: neither
rationale: Satirical criticism of political logic ('MAGA logic'), not an actual call for violence against a protected group.

label: hate
rationale: Matches the exact NCIC example: 'Kalenjin presidency is always a curse to the nation' declares a community categorically unfit to lead.
